full_text_rationale: Full text proposes LatentMem, a multi-agent memory framework with an experience bank storing raw historical trajectories and a learnable memory composer that retrieves and distills them for future tasks, evaluated on coding benchmarks including KodCode and BigCodeBench.
extractor_notes: Fresh search architecture extraction batch 010; controlled-comparison and quality status remain pending aggregate recomputation.
autores: Muxin Fu, Guibin Zhang, Xiangyuan Xue, Yafu Li, Zefeng He, Siyuan Huang, Xiaoye Qu, Yu Cheng, Yang Yang
titulo: LatentMem: Customizing Latent Memory for Multi-Agent Systems
formato_armazenamento: Experience bank stores raw multi-agent trajectories in lightweight form; memory composer retrieves trajectories and synthesizes fixed-length role-aware latent memory tokens conditioned on agent context; LMPO trains the composer from task-level rewards.
framework_agente: LatentMem learnable multi-agent memory framework with experience bank, role profiles, memory composer, latent memory injection, and LMPO training
metodo_avaliacao: benchmark; cross-domain generalization; cross-MAS generalization; ablation; sensitivity analysis; time and token cost analysis; fine-tuning comparison
benchmarks_utilizados: TriviaQA, KodCode, StrategyQA, PopQA, BigCodeBench, PDDL across AutoGen, MacNet, CAMEL, and DyLAN multi-agent frameworks
metricas_desempenho: task performance percentage, average improvement, held-in and held-out benchmark performance, time cost, token cost, latent memory length sensitivity, retrieval-count sensitivity
metricas_cl: Experience-bank real-time update ablation, role-aware memory ablation, 40,580 stored trajectories, latent token length L'=8, K retrieval sensitivity
baseline_comparado: No-memory, MetaGPT, Generative, Voyager, G-Memory, OAgent/OAgents, JoyAgent, ChatDev, MARTI fine-tuning baseline
tamanho_memoria_ao_longo_tempo: Training collection contains 40,580 trajectories from AutoGen and MacNet on TriviaQA, KodCode, StrategyQA, and PopQA. The chosen latent memory length is L'=8.
custo_por_tarefa: NR per task; figures report aggregate time and token consumption, for example LatentMem uses 1.09e4 seconds on MacNet+KodCode and 2.31e7 tokens on CAMEL+PopQA.
reducao_tokens: The paper reports about one-third fewer tokens and inference time reduced to about two-thirds relative to mainstream memory designs; it also reports lowest token usage among memory baselines in several plotted settings.
resultado_principal: LatentMem achieves up to 19.36% gain over vanilla settings and consistently outperforms existing memory architectures across diverse benchmarks and MAS frameworks.
desempenho_vs_baseline: In Table 4 with Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct, LatentMem improves AutoGen average from 52.86 to 61.82, MacNet from 53.38 to 61.00, CAMEL from 53.37 to 60.04, and DyLAN from 52.80 to 60.06; KodCode gains range from +14.90 to +18.45 points.
custo_vs_baseline: LatentMem is reported as fastest and lowest-token among memory baselines in the added cost figures; examples include 1.76e6 tokens on AutoGen+KodCode versus G-Memory at 3.04e6 and 1.09e4 seconds on MacNet+KodCode versus G-Memory at 2.02e4.
limitacoes: The paper emphasizes effectiveness and efficiency but still depends on collected in-domain trajectories, learned composer behavior, role-profile quality, and benchmark/MAS framework coverage; out-of-domain generalization is evaluated but not exhaustive.
ameacas_validade: Results may depend on synthetic coding and planning benchmarks, selected MAS frameworks, trajectory collection protocol, composer base model, LMPO reward design, latent-length and retrieval-count settings, and LLM-as-component implementation choices.
